channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Ivory Coast votes as Ouattara seeks a fourth term with key rivals sidelined
item.description: ABIDJAN, Ivory Coast (AP) — Voters in Ivory Coast cast ballots Saturday to elect a new leader as longtime President Alassane Ouattara seeks a fourth term, while his key contenders were barred from the race.
item.dc:creator: OPE ADETAYO and CHINEDU ASADU, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Trump rips Canada's 'crooked' anti-tariffs ad as possibly AI
item.description: President Trump took another swing at Canada late Friday, calling a controversial anti-tariff ad featuring excerpts from a former President Reagan speech, "crooked" and "dishonest." He also suggested that the video was "possibly" generated by artificial intelligence (AI).
item.dc:creator: Ryan Mancini, The Hill

item.title: Malaysia warns ASEAN neutrality eroding as global powers compete
item.description: KUALA LUMPUR, Malaysia (AP) — Malaysian foreign minister warned his counterparts from Southeast Asia that the space for neutrality is shrinking in a region increasingly shaped by big power rivalry, as they mark U.S. President Donald Trump’s first trip to Asia since returning to the White House.
item.dc:creator: EILEEN NG, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Trump travels to Asia ahead of meeting with President Xi
item.description: “We have a lot to talk about with President Xi, and he has a lot to talk about with us," Trump told reporters as he left the White House.
item.dc:creator: MICHELLE L. PRICE and MARK SCHIEFELBEIN, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Europe fights to avoid being sidelined by Trump’s world-shaping plans
item.description: Donald Trump has seized a central role on the global stage, leaving European allies on the sidelines.
item.dc:creator: Jill Lawless And Aamer Madhani, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Tropical Storm Melissa stationary in the Caribbean as 4 deaths reported and huge rains expected
item.description: SAN JUAN, Puerto Rico (AP) — Tropical Storm Melissa was nearly stationary in the central Caribbean on Friday as forecasters warned it could strengthen and swipe Jamaica as a powerful hurricane and dump a staggering amount of rain — up to 35 inches (89 centimeters) — on southwest Haiti, where they wa
item.dc:creator: DÁNICA COTO, JOHN MYERS JR. and EVENS SANON, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Japan's new leader faces diplomatic gauntlet with Trump, China and regional summits
item.description: Japan's new Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi faces significant foreign policy challenges just days after taking office.
item.dc:creator: Ken Moritsugu And Mari Yamaguchi, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Trump to meet Chinese president next week for key trade deal: White House
item.description: President Trump is preparing for a high-stakes trip to Asia aimed at testing his reputation as a dealmaker, with a potential trade agreement with China on the line.
item.dc:creator: DIDI TANG and PAUL WISEMAN, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: 48 bags of suspected human remains found at Mexican gravesite
item.description: Previous discoveries of bags found buried in Jalisco state gravesites have included bone fragments and human tissue.
item.dc:creator: Jeff Arnold, NewsNation

item.title: Trump hits Colombian President Petro with sanctions
item.description: The Trump administration leveled sanctions directly on Colombian President Gustavo Petro and his family members, escalating his feud with the South American leader.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, The Hill

item.title: Japan's new leader vows to further boost defense spending as regional tensions rise
item.description: TOKYO (AP) — Japanese Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi, in her first major policy speech on Friday, pledged to further accelerate Japan’s military buildup and spending and complete an early upgrade of the country’s security strategy as tensions rise with China, North Korea and Russia.
item.dc:creator: MARI YAMAGUCHI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Pentagon sends largest aircraft carrier to Caribbean amid boat strikes
item.description: The Pentagon is sending the world’s largest aircraft carrier, USS Gerald R. Ford, and its carrier air wing to the Caribbean as the Trump administration conducts strikes against boats it says are smuggling illegal drugs in the region.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, The Hill

item.title: Men sentenced over London arson attack masterminded by Russia as part of a wider sabotage campaign
item.description: LONDON (AP) — A British judge on Friday sentenced five men for their roles in an arson attack in London that authorities say was masterminded by Russian intelligence as part of a wider campaign of sabotage and disruption. Their sentences ranged from seven to 17 years in prison.
item.dc:creator: EMMA BURROWS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: US deploys Ford carrier strike group to combat narco-terror in Western Hemisphere
item.description: The Gerald R. Ford Carrier Strike Group is being deployed to continue targeting suspected drug smuggling boats in the Caribbean, the Pentagon said Friday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Ukraine's GOP allies look to seize Trump's momentum versus Putin
item.description: Senators are hoping to seize the momentum as President Trump goes on the offensive against Russian President Vladimir Putin, though Republicans remain cautious about getting ahead of the commander in chief.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, WGN 9

item.title: How a German freight lift became an unexpected social media star in the Louvre heist
item.description: BERLIN (AP) — Forget France: The Louvre Museum jewelry heist was a classic case of German efficiency. Photos of a German-made freight lift that the thieves used in a lightning-fast daylight heist at the Louvre have gone viral. And its manufacturer is now riding high from the unexpected endorsement.
item.dc:creator: STEFANIE DAZIO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Reporter’s Notebook: A Paris jewel heist straight out of the movies
item.description: A movie-style heist at the Louvre saw criminals scale the museum with a cherry picker truck and steal priceless French crown jewelry before fleeing on scooters.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Turkey in talks with Qatar and Oman to buy used Eurofighter jets, Erdogan says
item.description: ANKARA, Turkey (AP) — Turkey is negotiating with Qatar and Oman to acquire used Eurofighter Typhoon fighter jets as part of its effort to bolster its air force capabilities, President Recep Tayyip Erdogan said in comments released Friday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Tanzania's first female president has defied early hopes that she would ease repression
item.description: KAMPALA, Uganda (AP) — Less than six months after Samia Suluhu Hassan was inaugurated as Tanzania’s first woman president in 2021, the country’s most famous cartoonist depicted her in a military general’s uniform under the title “Dictatoress.
item.dc:creator: RODNEY MUHUMUZA and EVELYNE MUSAMBI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Ukraine’s Zelenskyy urges US to broaden Russian oil sanctions and seeks long-range missiles
item.description: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy has urged the United States to expand sanctions on Russia’s two biggest oil companies to the whole sector.
item.dc:creator: Jill Lawless, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Tropical Storm Melissa lumbers through the Caribbean, killing an elderly man in Haiti
item.description: SAN JUAN, Puerto Rico (AP) — Tropical Storm Melissa lumbered through the Caribbean Sea on Thursday, bringing a risk of dangerous landslides and life-threatening flooding to Jamaica and southern Hispaniola — an island shared by the Dominican Republic and Haiti.
item.dc:creator: DÁNICA COTO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump cuts off trade talks with Canada over Reagan tariff ad
item.description: President Trump said Thursday he was cutting off trade negotiations with Canada, citing an ad campaign that used former President Reagan’s warnings about the long-term risks of tariffs amid the trade dispute between the U.S. and Canada.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, NewsNation

item.title: Trump says declaration of war not needed for strikes on alleged cartel boats
item.description: President Trump on Thursday dismissed the need to ask Congress for a declaration of war as the U.S. military continuously launches strikes on boats in the Caribbean Sea and Pacific Ocean. “I don’t think we’re going to necessarily ask for a declaration of war,” Trump told reporters.
item.dc:creator: Max Rego, The Hill

item.title: Trump-Kim meeting speculation flares ahead of US president's visit to South Korea
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — The last time U.S. President Donald Trump visited South Korea in 2019, he made a surprise trip to the border with North Korea for an impromptu meeting with North Korean leader Kim Jong Un to revive faltering nuclear talks.
item.dc:creator: HYUNG-JIN KIM and KIM TONG-HYUNG, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Albania’s Prince Leka engaged
item.description: The country’s crown prince has finally popped the question to his longtime girlfriend, photographer Blerta Celibashi.
item.dc:creator: Paula Froelich, NewsNation

item.title: Putin says Russia won't bow to the US
item.description: President Vladimir Putin cautioned that it would deliver an “overwhelming” response to any military strikes deep inside Russia.
item.dc:creator: By Ksenia Orlova and Maxim Rodionov, NewsNation

item.title: Louvre director grilled on spectacular security failures, including camera pointing away from key balcony
item.description: Louvre Museum director Laurence des Cars acknowledged that security failures, including a lack of perimeter surveillance, allowed thieves to pull off heist.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Rubio will travel to Israel to ensure fragile Gaza ceasefire holds
item.description: Secretary of State Rubio plans to visit a new civilian military coordination center in Israel, which was visited by Vice President JD Vance earlier this week.
item.dc:creator: RENATA BRITO and JOSEF FEDERMAN, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: PM Mark Carney says Canada will double its non-US exports
item.description: President Trump has been threatening Canada’s economy and sovereignty with tariffs, most offensively by claiming Canada could be “the 51st state.
item.dc:creator: ROB GILLIES, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Woman charged in $1.7M gold heist at Paris museum: Report
item.description: Investigators revealed the woman had left France the day of the break-in and was preparing to return to China.
item.dc:creator: Rob Taub, NewsNation

item.title: Putin directs drills of Russian nuclear forces as his summit with Trump is put on hold
item.description: MOSCOW (AP) — Russian President Vladimir Putin on Wednesday directed drills of the country’s strategic nuclear forces that featured practice missile launches, an exercise that came as his planned summit on Ukraine with U.S. President Donald Trump was put on hold.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Iraq bans Roblox over child safety concerns
item.description: Iraq has joined several countries — including China, Turkey, Oman and Qatar — to ban U.S. user-generated video game platform Roblox over child safety.
item.dc:creator: Cher Hwang, NewsNation

item.title: Trump pick for religious freedom envoy visits Holy Land, cites strong US-Israel bond
item.description: Baptist pastor Mark Walker discusses U.S.-Israel spiritual bond and plans to combat global religious persecution after Trump nomination as international religious freedom ambassador.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: How Louvre burglars obtained truck-mounted lift to make off with jewels worth more than $100M
item.description: Thieves used a stolen truck-mounted moving lift to scale the Louvre and steal royal jewels worth over $100 million in a lightning-fast Paris heist.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: US military opens coordination center in Israel for Gaza stabilization and aid efforts amid ceasefire
item.description: The U.S. military created a Civil-Military Coordination Center near Gaza to oversee ceasefire implementation and facilitate humanitarian assistance from international partners.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Jane Goodall's cause of death revealed: Report
item.description: Epilepsy was also listed as one of Goodall's conditions, though it's not clear if it contributed to her death.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, NewsNation

item.title: Israel resumes ceasefire in Gaza and says aid deliveries will restart Monday
item.description: JERUSALEM (AP) — Gaza's fragile ceasefire faced its first major test Sunday as Israeli forces launched a wave of deadly strikes, saying Hamas militants had killed two soldiers, and an Israeli security official said the transfer of aid into the territory was halted.
item.dc:creator: JOSEF FEDERMAN and SAMY MAGDY, Associated Press, WFLA, NBC
